overview.description: AI-powered product adoption platform for user onboarding and digital adoption
overview.description_long: Product Fruits is an AI-driven digital adoption platform (DAP) that helps SaaS product teams create effective in-app onboarding flows, product tours, hints, tooltips, NPS surveys, and knowledge bases — all without coding. Their Elvin AI copilot autonomously resolves support questions, generates personalized onboarding flows, and guides users to activation through adaptive, conversational onboarding. Trusted by 1,300+ teams including Sanofi, Rakuten, and Mango.

history.origin_story: Founded in June 2019 by Ladislav Šalom and Karel Papik in Prague to help SaaS teams deliver effective user onboarding without coding.

key_milestones.event: Raised €1.6M seed round led by Leverage and Venture to Future Fund
Raised additional €1.47M led by Reflex Capital, total funding €3.22M
Launched Elvin AI copilot for autonomous onboarding and support ticket reduction
Rebranded as 'The AI-Powered Product Adoption Platform'; serves 1,300+ teams

risk_assessment.agi_displacement_rationale: Product Fruits wraps AI capabilities (Elvin AI copilot) around a product adoption layer with in-app integration, analytics, segmentation, and no-code workflow tooling. The core onboarding content generation could be commoditized by foundation models, but the deep in-app integration layer, behavioral analytics, and enterprise SaaS integrations provide some moat. Competitors are also racing to add AI, increasing the risk.
risk_assessment.market_risk_rationale: The digital adoption platform (DAP) market is established and growing, with real paying customers (1,300+ teams) including enterprise brands like Sanofi and Rakuten. Product-led growth and onboarding automation are mainstream SaaS priorities, providing sustained demand.
risk_assessment.funding_risk_rationale: Total funding of ~€3.22M is modest (seed stage), but the company has 1,300+ paying customers generating SaaS subscription revenue, reducing dependency on venture capital. Plans to expand to US and European markets may require additional funding.
risk_assessment.overall_risk_rationale: Product Fruits has genuine traction with 1,300+ customers and real revenue, but operates in a competitive space (Pendo, WalkMe, Userpilot) where AI is increasingly table stakes. The modest funding base and mid-market positioning create execution risk even as market demand is solid.

metadata.notes: Founded June 2019 per LinkedIn/TheOrg (Tracxn also confirms 2019); LinkedIn company page shows 'Founded 2021' which may reflect a rebranding or legal entity change. Funding total of ~$3.5M / €3.22M confirmed by multiple sources. 33 employees per RocketReach, 29 on LinkedIn (March 2026).
